marketState: neutral-to-bullish, weather-led upside fighting a storage-surplus/technical drag
summary: Weather demand is running well above the 5-year normal and building further into the 8-16 day window (+7.2% then +13.2%), a genuine bullish driver in this Cooling-season stretch. Fair value itself is rising over the next 3 weeks ($2.93 to $3.08) and the front contract, while modestly rich now (+$0.28, SLIGHTLY OVER), sits inside its band and converges toward FAIR by plus14D/21D, so it is not stretched. Managed money is net short at just the 18th percentile of its 5-year range, an extreme that argues for limited further downside and squeeze risk rather than trend continuation lower. Against that, this week's storage build beat forecast (+87 vs +74 Bcf, a bearish surprise) and the surplus now sits +175 Bcf above the 5-year average, while daily/weekly technicals trend bearish even though intraday frames are flat with no overbought/oversold extremes. Net: a modest bullish lean for the multi-week horizon on weather/seasonality/positioning, but no clean short-term trigger given mixed technicals and missing futures-curve data.

longTerm.stop: $2.85 (below the now-fair-value estimate of $2.93 and the plus7D band's lower support)
longTerm.tp2: $3.85 (seasonal Sep-strength extension into peak cooling demand)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; take partial profit (about half) at TP1, trail the rest to TP2
longTerm.rationale: Weather demand is accelerating above 5-year normal into the 8-16 day window (+13.2%), seasonality shows 63% odds of higher prices in 4 weeks (reliability only Low), and the fair-value curve itself is rising ($2.93 -> $3.08) while price stays inside band. Managed-money net shorts at the 18th 5y percentile add contrarian upside fuel, though the +175 Bcf storage surplus and bigger-than-forecast build are a real headwind that caps conviction at medium.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily close below ~$2.85 (under current fair value), weather forecasts reverting to/below 5-year normal, or storage surprises staying bearish for multiple weeks would confirm the supply glut dominates and invalidate the long.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather (demand_1_7D +7.2% vs 5y) is mildly supportive but far less stretched than the 8-16 day window, and price is only modestly rich to fair ($3.21 vs $2.93 fair, still SLIGHTLY OVER not extreme). Technicals are genuinely split: 5m/15m/1h/4h trend neutral with mixed EMA crosses and MACD, only 1d/1w lean bearish, and RSI sits mid-range (44-53) on every timeframe with no Bollinger breakout. The futuresCurve dataset needed to confirm contango/backwardation dynamics is entirely missing (null), removing a normally-weighted short-term input, so conviction is low and a clean trigger is absent.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback to the $3.00-3.05 plus7D fair-value zone to add longs, or a bullish confirmation (1h and 4h EMA turning bullish together with RSI>55 and MACD histogram flipping positive) before entering; a daily close below $2.90 would instead favor a short-term short back toward fair value.

Fair value rising over the next 3 weeks ($2.93 -> $3.08) while front price stays inside its band, only modestly rich

risks: Seasonality reliability is rated Low (51pp historical spread), so the 63% 4-week-higher odds carry wide uncertainty
